system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

    version     2.0;

application     rhoSimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

method          scotch;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvConstraints', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - inlet straight duct (along +x)
    (-50  25 -25)   // 0
    (-50  -25 -25)  // 1
    (  0  -25 -25)  // 2
    (  0   25 -25)  // 3

    (-50  25  25)   // 4
    (-50  -25  25)  // 5
    (  0  -25  25)  // 6
    (  0   25  25)  // 7

    // Block 1 - bend section (L-shaped in xy-plane)
    // Bottom face at z = -25
    (  0   25 -25)  // 8 (shared with Block 0 vertex 3)
    (  0  -50 -25)  // 9
    ( 25  -50 -25)  // 10
    ( 25 -125 -25)  // 11

    // Top face at z =  25
    (  0   25  25)  // 12 (shared with Block 0 vertex 7)
    (  0  -50  25)  // 13
    ( 25  -50  25)  // 14
    ( 25 -125  25)  // 15

    // Block 2 - outlet straight duct (along -y)
    (-500 -125 -25)  // 16
    (-500 -125  25)  // 17
    (  25 -125 -25)  // 18 (shared with Block 1 vertex 11)
    (  25 -125  25)  // 19 (shared with Block 1 vertex 15)

    (-500  -75 -25)  // 20
    (-500  -75  25)  // 21
    (  25  -75 -25)  // 22 (shared with Block 1 vertex 14)
    (  25  -75  25)  // 23 (shared with Block 1 vertex 15? No, 15 is at -125)
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight duct (along +x)
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Bend section
    hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Outlet straight duct (along -y)
    hex (16 17 18 19 20 21 22 23) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 5 1)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (20 21 23 22)
        );
    }

    upperWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            // Block 0 top
            (4 7 6 5)
            // Block 1 top
            (12 13 14 15)
            // Block 2 top
            (21 20 22 23)
        );
    }

    lowerWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            // Block 0 bottom
            (0 3 2 1)
            // Block 1 bottom
            (8 11 10 9)
            // Block 2 bottom
            (16 17 21 20)
        );
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            // Block 0 left (+z side)
            (0 4 7 3)
            // Block 1 left (+z side)
            (8 12 15 11)
            // Block 2 left (+z side)
            (16 21 20 17)
        );
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            // Block 0 right (-z side)
            (1 5 6 2)
            // Block 1 right (-z side)
            (9 10 14 13)
            // Block 2 right (-z side)
            (18 19 23 22)
        );
    }

    frontWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            // Block 1 front (at y=-125)
            (11 15 14 10)
            // Block 2 front (at y=-125)
            (18 19 23 22)
        );
    }

    backWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            // Block 1 back (at x=0)
            (8 9 13 12)
            // Block 2 back (at x=-500)
            (16 17 21 20)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //

    From function virtual Foam::IOobject Foam::fileOperation::findInstance(const Foam::IOobject&, Foam::scalar, const Foam::word&) const
    in file global/fileOperations/fileOperation/fileOperation.C at line 869.

FOAM exiting'}, {'file': 'log.blockMesh', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Block hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) has inward-pointing faces
    4(11 15 14 10) 

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_C_Cmodels/runs/squareBend/1/system/blockMeshDict/blocks at line 56.

    From function void Foam::blockDescriptor::check(const Foam::Istream&)
    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 106.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors

Two persistent errors:

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: `Block hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15) ... has inward-pointing faces`
2. **`log.rhoSimpleFoam`**: Cascading error — cannot find mesh because blockMesh failed

## Root Cause

The fundamental problem is the **multi-block vertex layout**. Looking at the current `blockMeshDict` carefully:

- **Block 0**: `hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)` — vertices 0(-50,25,-25) to 7(0,25,25). This defines a proper rectangular prism from x=-50..0, y=-25..25, z=-25..25 ✓

- **Block 1**: `hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15)` — vertices 8(0,25,-25)/9(0,-50,-25)/10(25,-50,-25)/11(25,-125,-25) and 12(0,25,25)/13(0,-50,25)/14(25,-50,25)/15(25,-125,25). **All vertices have either x=0 or x=25.** This means the block collapses into a 2D sheet — it has zero thickness in the x-direction between x=0 and x=25, but the vertices don't span intermediate x-values. More critically, the face `(8 12 15 11)` (which should be a face of the block) evaluates to `4(8 12 15 11)` in the error — this face consists of vertices all at x=0 or x=25, meaning it lies in a plane and points inward.

- **Block 2**: `hex (16 17 18 19 20 21 22 23)` — vertices 16(-500,-125,-25), 17(-500,-125,25), 18(25,-125,-25), 19(25,-125,25), 20(-500,-75,-25), 21(-500,-75,25), 22(25,-75,-25), 23(25,-75,25). Again, vertices 18,19,22,23 are all at x=25 and vertices 16,17,20,21 are at x=-500. Same degeneracy issue.

## The Real Problem

The vertex layout tries to force a multi-block topology where **Blocks 1 and 2 are geometrically degenerate** — they collapse into 2D faces because all vertices lie on only two x-planes (x=0 and x=25 for Block 1, x=-500 and x=25 for Block 2). Additionally, the boundary face definitions reference faces that may be internal or don't match the actual block exteriors.

## Recommended Solution — Different Approach from Previous Attempts

Since previous attempts at fixing vertex ordering haven't resolved the geometric degeneracy, **redesign the entire mesh to use a single hex block** (or a proper non-degenerate multi-block layout):

1. **Single-Block Approach**: Create one hex block that spans the full domain approximately from x=-500 to x=25, y=-125 to y=25, z=-25 to z=25. Apply grading so that the region near x=0 (the bend) has finer resolution. The boundary patches would be:
   - `inlet`: face at x=-50 (subset of the left face)
   - `outlet`: face at x=-500 (subset of the right face)
   - Remaining external faces as walls

2. **Proper Multi-Block Alternative**: If keeping multiple blocks is essential, restructure completely:
   - **Block A**: Inlet duct from x=-50 to x=0 (full y-range -25..25, z=-25..25)
   - **Block B**: Transition/bend region from x=0 to x=25 and y=25 down to y=-125 (so it occupies x=0..25 and y=-125..25)
   - **Block C**: Outlet duct extending from y=-125 upward/downward

   The key difference: Block B must span a **true 3D volume** — its vertices must cover a range of x-values (not just x=0 and x=25) and y-values. Specifically, the bend block should connect the end of Block A at x=0 to extend in the y-direction toward y=-125, with z-variation throughout.

3. **Boundary Definitions**: After restructuring the blocks, the boundary face definitions must exactly match the exposed external faces of the assembled blocks. Every face listed under `inlet`, `outlet`, `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall` must correspond to a face that belongs to exactly one block and is on the exterior of the mesh.

The core fix is eliminating the degenerate blocks. Either use a single hex block with graded mesh, or restructure as a proper multi-block where every block spans a genuine 3D volume (no two opposing vertices sharing identical x, y, or z coordinates that collapse the block).</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Redesign entire mesh as a single hex block to avoid degenerate multi-block geometry; Define vertices spanning x=-500 to x=25, y=-125 to y=25, z=-25 to z=25; Use 10 cells in x, 10 cells in y, 10 cells in z with appropriate grading concentrated near the bend region around x=0 to x=25 and y=-125; Set inlet patch as the face at x=-50 covering full y-z extent; Set outlet patch as the face at x=-500 covering full y-z extent; All remaining external faces become wall patches named upperWall, lowerWall, leftWall, rightWall, frontWall, backWall matching the boundary face definitions already present'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
